v.CNV: 耶和华对摩西说：
v.ESV: The Lord spoke to Moses, saying,
v.GAE: 여호와께서 모세에게 말씀하여 이르시되
v.KJV: And the LORD spake unto Moses, saying,
v.NASB: The LORD spoke further to Moses, saying,
v.NIV: The Lord said to Moses:
v.NRSV: The LORD spoke to Moses, saying:
v.SAENEW: 주님께서 모세에게 말씀하셨다.
v.WLB: 여호와께서 모세에게 말씀하셨습니다.

v.CNV: “你要用银子作两枝号，要锤出来的，可以用来召集会众，也可以叫会众拔营起行。
v.ESV: “Make two silver trumpets. Of hammered work you shall make them, and you shall use them for summoning the congregation and for breaking camp.
v.GAE: 은 나팔 둘을 만들되 두들겨 만들어서 그것으로 회중을 소집하며 진영을 출발하게 할 것이라
v.KJV: Make thee two trumpets of silver; of a whole piece shalt thou make them: that thou mayest use them for the calling of the assembly, and for the journeying of the camps.
v.NASB: 'Make yourself two trumpets of silver, of hammered work you shall make them; and you shall use them for summoning the congregation and for having the camps set out.
v.NIV: “Make two trumpets of hammered silver, and use them for calling the community together and for having the camps set out.
v.NRSV: Make two silver trumpets; you shall make them of hammered work; and you shall use them for summoning the congregation, and for breaking camp.
v.SAENEW: "너는 은나팔 두 개를 만들되 은을 두드려서 만들어라. 그것들은 네가 회중을 불러모을 때와 진을 출발시킬 때에 필요한 것들이다.
v.WLB: “은을 두드려서 나팔 두 개를 만들고 회중을 불러 모을 때와 진을 거두고 행군을 시작할 때 그것을 사용하여라.

v.CNV: 吹号的时候，全体会众都要到你那里，聚集在会幕的门口。
v.ESV: And when both are blown, all the congregation shall gather themselves to you at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
v.GAE: 나팔 두 개를 불 때에는 온 회중이 회막 문 앞에 모여서 네게로 나아올 것이요
v.KJV: And when they shall blow with them, all the assembly shall assemble themselves to thee at the door of the tabernacle of the congregation.
v.NASB: 'When both are blown, all the congregation shall gather themselves to you at the doorway of the tent of meeting.
v.NIV: When both are sounded, the whole community is to assemble before you at the entrance to the tent of meeting.
v.NRSV: When both are blown, the whole congregation shall assemble before you at the entrance of the tent of meeting.
v.SAENEW: 두 개를 한꺼번에 길게 불면, 모든 회중이 회막 어귀에 모여 너에게로 나올 것이다.
v.WLB: 나팔을 둘 다 불면 온 회중이 회막 입구 너에게로 모여야 한다.

v.CNV: 如果单吹一枝，众领袖，就是以色列的族长，都要到你那里聚集。
v.ESV: But if they blow only one, then the chiefs, the heads of the tribes of Israel, shall gather themselves to you.
v.GAE: 하나만 불 때에는 이스라엘의 천부장 된 지휘관들이 모여서 네게로 나아올 것이며
v.KJV: And if they blow but with one trumpet, then the princes, which are heads of the thousands of Israel, shall gather themselves unto thee.
v.NASB: 'Yet if only one is blown, then the leaders, the heads of the divisions of Israel, shall assemble before you.
v.NIV: If only one is sounded, the leaders —the heads of the clans of Israel—are to assemble before you.
v.NRSV: But if only one is blown, then the leaders, the heads of the tribes of Israel, shall assemble before you.
v.SAENEW: 그러나 하나만 길게 불면, 지휘관들, 곧 이스라엘의 천부장들만이 너에게로 나올 것이다.
v.WLB: 나팔을 하나만 불면 지도자들, 곧 이스라엘의 천부장들만 너에게로 모여야 한다.

v.CNV: 你们吹大声的时候，那在东边的营，都要起行。
v.ESV: When you blow an alarm, the camps that are on the east side shall set out.
v.GAE: 너희가 그것을 크게 불 때에는 동쪽 진영들이 행진할 것이며
v.KJV: When ye blow an alarm, then the camps that lie on the east parts shall go forward.
v.NASB: 'But when you blow an alarm, the camps that are pitched on the east side shall set out.
v.NIV: When a trumpet blast is sounded, the tribes camping on the east are to set out.
v.NRSV: When you blow an alarm, the camps on the east side shall set out;
v.SAENEW: 그러나 나팔을 짧게 급히 불면, 동쪽에 진을 친 부대들이 진을 뜬다.
v.WLB: 짧게 끊어서 부는 나팔소리가 나면 동쪽 진영이 움직인다.

v.CNV: 你们第二次吹大声的时候，那在南边的营，都要起行；他们要起行，必须吹大声。
v.ESV: And when you blow an alarm the second time, the camps that are on the south side shall set out. An alarm is to be blown whenever they are to set out.
v.GAE: 두 번째로 크게 불 때에는 남쪽 진영들이 행진할 것이라 떠나려 할 때에는 나팔 소리를 크게 불 것이며
v.KJV: When ye blow an alarm the second time, then the camps that lie on the south side shall take their journey: they shall blow an alarm for their journeys.
v.NASB: 'When you blow an alarm the second time, the camps that are pitched on the south side shall set out; an alarm is to be blown for them to set out.
v.NIV: At the sounding of a second blast, the camps on the south are to set out. The blast will be the signal for setting out.
v.NRSV: when you blow a second alarm, the camps on the south side shall set out. An alarm is to be blown whenever they are to set out.
v.SAENEW: 두 번째로 짧게 불면, 남쪽에 진을 친 부대들이 진을 뜬다. 진을 뜰 때에는 나팔을 이렇게 짧게 불어라.
v.WLB: 두 번째로 짧게 끊어서 부는 나팔소리가 나면 남쪽 진영이 움직인다. 짧게 끊어서 부는 나팔소리는 움직인다는 신호다.

v.CNV: 但召集会众的时候，你们要吹号，却不要吹大声。
v.ESV: But when the assembly is to be gathered together, you shall blow a long blast, but you shall not sound an alarm.
v.GAE: 또 회중을 모을 때에도 나팔을 불 것이나 소리를 크게 내지 말며
v.KJV: But when the congregation is to be gathered together, ye shall blow, but ye shall not sound an alarm.
v.NASB: 'When convening the assembly, however, you shall blow without sounding an alarm.
v.NIV: To gather the assembly, blow the trumpets, but not with the signal for setting out.
v.NRSV: But when the assembly is to be gathered, you shall blow, but you shall not sound an alarm.
v.SAENEW: 총회를 소집할 때에는 나팔을 길게 불어야 한다. 짧게 불어서는 안 된다.
v.WLB: 회중을 모으기 위해서는 길게 나팔을 불어야지 짧게 끊어 불어서는 안 된다.

v.CNV: 亚伦的子孙作祭司的要作吹号的；这要作你们世世代代永远的条例。
v.ESV: And the sons of Aaron, the priests, shall blow the trumpets. The trumpets shall be to you for a perpetual statute throughout your generations.
v.GAE: 그 나팔은 아론의 자손인 제사장들이 불지니 이는 너희 대대에 영원한 율례니라
v.KJV: And the sons of Aaron, the priests, shall blow with the trumpets; and they shall be to you for an ordinance for ever throughout your generations.
v.NASB: 'The priestly sons of Aaron, moreover, shall blow the trumpets; and this shall be for you a perpetual statute throughout your generations.
v.NIV: “The sons of Aaron, the priests, are to blow the trumpets. This is to be a lasting ordinance for you and the generations to come.
v.NRSV: The sons of Aaron, the priests, shall blow the trumpets; this shall be a perpetual institution for you throughout your generations.
v.SAENEW: 나팔은 아론의 혈통을 이어받은 제사장들만이 불 수 있다.이것은 너희가 대대로 길이 지킬 율례이다.
v.WLB: 나팔은 제사장인 아론의 아들들이 불어야 한다. 이것은 너희와 너희 대대로 계속될 규례다.

v.CNV: 你们在本地与欺压你们的仇敌作战的时候，就要吹大声，使你们在耶和华你们的　神面前得蒙记念，也得拯救脱离你们的仇敌。
v.ESV: And when you go to war in your land against the adversary who oppresses you, then you shall sound an alarm with the trumpets, that you may be remembered before the Lord your God, and you shall be saved from your enemies.
v.GAE: 또 너희 땅에서 너희가 자기를 압박하는 대적을 치러 나갈 때에는 나팔을 크게 불지니 그리하면 너희 하나님 여호와가 너희를 기억하고 너희를 너희의 대적에게서 구원하시리라
v.KJV: And if ye go to war in your land against the enemy that oppresseth you, then ye shall blow an alarm with the trumpets; and ye shall be remembered before the LORD your God, and ye shall be saved from your enemies.
v.NASB: 'When you go to war in your land against the adversary who attacks you, then you shall sound an alarm with the trumpets, that you may be remembered before the LORD your God, and be saved from your enemies.
v.NIV: When you go into battle in your own land against an enemy who is oppressing you, sound a blast on the trumpets. Then you will be remembered by the Lord your God and rescued from your enemies.
v.NRSV: When you go to war in your land against the adversary who oppresses you, you shall sound an alarm with the trumpets, so that you may be remembered before the LORD your God and be saved from your enemies.
v.SAENEW: 너희의 땅에서 너희를 공격해 온 침략자들에 대항하여 전쟁에 나설 때에는, 나팔을 짧게 급히 불어라. 그러면 주 너희의 하나님이 너희를 기억하고, 너희 원수들에게서 너희를 구해 줄 것이다.
v.WLB: 너희가 너희 땅에서 너희를 공격하는 적과 싸울 때 나팔을 불어라. 그러면 너희가 너희 하나님 여호와 앞에서 기억될 것이며 너희 원수들에게서 구원을 받게 될 것이다.

v.CNV: 此外，在你们快乐的日子和指定的节期，以及月朔，你们献燔祭和平安祭的时候，也要吹号，这都要在你们的　神面前替你们作为记念；我是耶和华你们的　神。”
v.ESV: On the day of your gladness also, and at your appointed feasts and at the beginnings of your months, you shall blow the trumpets over your burnt offerings and over the sacrifices of your peace offerings. They shall be a reminder of you before your God: I am the Lord your God.”
v.GAE: 또 너희의 희락의 날과 너희가 정한 절기와 초하루에는 번제물을 드리고 화목제물을 드리며 나팔을 불라 그로 말미암아 너희의 하나님이 너희를 기억하시리라 나는 너희의 하나님 여호와니라
v.KJV: Also in the day of your gladness, and in your solemn days, and in the beginnings of your months, ye shall blow with the trumpets over your burnt offerings, and over the sacrifices of your peace offerings; that they may be to you for a memorial before your God: I am the LORD your God.
v.NASB: 'Also in the day of your gladness and in your appointed feasts, and on the first days of your months, you shall blow the trumpets over your burnt offerings, and over the sacrifices of your peace offerings; and they shall be as a reminder of you before your God. I am the LORD your God.'
v.NIV: Also at your times of rejoicing—your appointed festivals and New Moon feasts —you are to sound the trumpets over your burnt offerings and fellowship offerings, and they will be a memorial for you before your God. I am the Lord your God. ”
v.NRSV: Also on your days of rejoicing, at your appointed festivals, and at the beginnings of your months, you shall blow the trumpets over your burnt offerings and over your sacrifices of well-being; they shall serve as a reminder on your behalf before the LORD your God: I am the LORD your God.
v.SAENEW: 너희들이 즐기는 경축일과 너희들이 정기적으로 모이는 날과 매달 초하루에는, 너희가 번제물과 화목제물을 바치며 나팔을 불어라. 그러면 너희 주 하나님이 너희를 기억할 것이다. 내가 주 너희의 하나님이다."
v.WLB: 또한 너희가 즐거울 때와 너희의 정해진 절기와 매달 초하루에 번제와 화목제를 드릴 때 나팔을 불어라. 그러면 너희 하나님이 너희를 기억할 것이다. 나는 너희 하나님 여호와다.”

v.CNV: 第二年二月二十日，云彩从法柜的帐幕收上去的时候，
v.ESV: In the second year, in the second month, on the twentieth day of the month, the cloud lifted from over the tabernacle of the testimony,
v.GAE: 둘째 해 둘째 달 스무날에 구름이 증거의 성막에서 떠오르매
v.KJV: And it came to pass on the twentieth day of the second month, in the second year, that the cloud was taken up from off the tabernacle of the testimony.
v.NASB: Now in the second year, in the second month, on the twentieth of the month, the cloud was lifted from over the tabernacle of the testimony;
v.NIV: On the twentieth day of the second month of the second year, the cloud lifted from above the tabernacle of the covenant law.
v.NRSV: In the second year, in the second month, on the twentieth day of the month, the cloud lifted from over the tabernacle of the covenant.
v.SAENEW: 제 이년 둘째 달, 그 달 이십일에 증거궤가 보관된 그 성막에서 비로소 구름이 걷혔다.
v.WLB: 2년째 되는 해 둘째 달 20일에 구름이 증거의 성막 위에서 나아가기 시작했습니다.

v.CNV: 以色列人就从西奈旷野按站起行；云彩在巴兰旷野停住了。
v.ESV: and the people of Israel set out by stages from the wilderness of Sinai. And the cloud settled down in the wilderness of Paran.
v.GAE: 이스라엘 자손이 시내 광야에서 출발하여 자기 길을 가더니 바란 광야에 구름이 머무니라
v.KJV: And the children of Israel took their journeys out of the wilderness of Sinai; and the cloud rested in the wilderness of Paran.
v.NASB: and the sons of Israel set out on their journeys from the wilderness of Sinai. Then the cloud settled down in the wilderness of Paran.
v.NIV: Then the Israelites set out from the Desert of Sinai and traveled from place to place until the cloud came to rest in the Desert of Paran.
v.NRSV: Then the Israelites set out by stages from the wilderness of Sinai, and the cloud settled down in the wilderness of Paran.
v.SAENEW: 이스라엘 자손은, 시내 광야를 떠나서 구름이 바란 광야에 머물 때까지, 여러 곳을 거쳐 행군을 계속하였다.
v.WLB: 그러자 이스라엘 자손들이 시내 광야를 떠나 행선지를 향해 나아갔습니다. 그러다 구름이 바란 광야에 머물렀습니다.

v.CNV: 这是他们第一次照着耶和华借摩西吩咐他们的起程。
v.ESV: They set out for the first time at the command of the Lord by Moses.
v.GAE: 이와 같이 그들이 여호와께서 모세에게 명령하신 것을 따라 행진하기를 시작하였는데
v.KJV: And they first took their journey according to the commandment of the LORD by the hand of Moses.
v.NASB: So they moved out for the first time according to the commandment of the LORD through Moses.
v.NIV: They set out, this first time, at the Lord ’s command through Moses.
v.NRSV: They set out for the first time at the command of the LORD by Moses.
v.SAENEW: 이것은 주님께서 모세를 시켜 지시하신 명령을 따라서 한 첫 번째 행군이었다.
v.WLB: 이렇게 그들은 여호와께서 모세를 통해 주신 명령대로 처음 길을 나섰습니다.

v.CNV: 犹大营的旗号，按着他们的队伍，首先起行；统领队伍的，是亚米拿达的儿子拿顺。
v.ESV: The standard of the camp of the people of Judah set out first by their companies, and over their company was Nahshon the son of Amminadab.
v.GAE: 선두로 유다 자손의 진영의 군기에 속한 자들이 그들의 진영별로 행진하였으니 유다 군대는 암미나답의 아들 나손이 이끌었고
v.KJV: In the first place went the standard of the camp of the children of Judah according to their armies: and over his host was Nahshon the son of Amminadab.
v.NASB: The standard of the camp of the sons of Judah, according to their armies, set out first, with Nahshon the son of Amminadab, over its army,
v.NIV: The divisions of the camp of Judah went first, under their standard. Nahshon son of Amminadab was in command.
v.NRSV: The standard of the camp of Judah set out first, company by company, and over the whole company was Nahshon son of Amminadab.
v.SAENEW: 맨 앞에는 유다 자손이 진의 부대기를 앞세우고, 부대별로 정렬하여 출발하였다. 유다 부대는 암미나답의 아들 나손이 이끌었고,
v.WLB: 가장 먼저 유다 진영의 깃발이 그 소속된 부대들을 이끌고 나아갔습니다. 그들은 암미나답의 아들 나손이 지휘했습니다.

v.CNV: 统领以萨迦支派队伍的，是苏押的儿子拿坦业。
v.ESV: And over the company of the tribe of the people of Issachar was Nethanel the son of Zuar.
v.GAE: 잇사갈 자손 지파의 군대는 수알의 아들 느다넬이 이끌었고
v.KJV: And over the host of the tribe of the children of Issachar was Nethaneel the son of Zuar.
v.NASB: and Nethanel the son of Zuar, over the tribal army of the sons of Issachar;
v.NIV: Nethanel son of Zuar was over the division of the tribe of Issachar,
v.NRSV: Over the company of the tribe of Issachar was Nethanel son of Zuar;
v.SAENEW: 뒤이어 따라나선 잇사갈 자손 지파 부대는 수알의 아들 느다넬이 이끌었고,
v.WLB: 잇사갈 지파의 부대는 수알의 아들 느다넬이 통솔했습니다.

v.CNV: 统领西布伦支派队伍的，是希伦的儿子以利押。
v.ESV: And over the company of the tribe of the people of Zebulun was Eliab the son of Helon.
v.GAE: 스불론 자손 지파의 군대는 헬론의 아들 엘리압이 이끌었더라
v.KJV: And over the host of the tribe of the children of Zebulun was Eliab the son of Helon.
v.NASB: and Eliab the son of Helon over the tribal army of the sons of Zebulun.
v.NIV: and Eliab son of Helon was over the division of the tribe of Zebulun.
v.NRSV: and over the company of the tribe of Zebulun was Eliab son of Helon.
v.SAENEW: 그 다음에 나선 스불론 자손 지파 부대는 헬론의 아들 엘리압이 이끌었다.
v.WLB: 스불론 지파의 부대는 헬론의 아들 엘리압이 통솔했습니다.

v.CNV: 帐幕拆下来以后，革顺的子孙和米拉利的子孙，就抬着帐幕起行。
v.ESV: And when the tabernacle was taken down, the sons of Gershon and the sons of Merari, who carried the tabernacle, set out.
v.GAE: 이에 성막을 걷으매 게르손 자손과 므라리 자손이 성막을 메고 출발하였으며
v.KJV: And the tabernacle was taken down; and the sons of Gershon and the sons of Merari set forward, bearing the tabernacle.
v.NASB: Then the tabernacle was taken down; and the sons of Gershon and the sons of Merari, who were carrying the tabernacle, set out.
v.NIV: Then the tabernacle was taken down, and the Gershonites and Merarites, who carried it, set out.
v.NRSV: Then the tabernacle was taken down, and the Gershonites and the Merarites, who carried the tabernacle, set out.
v.SAENEW: 뒤따라 성막 운반을 맡은 게르손 자손과 므라리 자손이 성막을 걷어 가지고 출발하였다.
v.WLB: 성막이 거두어지자 게르손 자손과 므라리 자손이 그것을 메고 길을 나섰습니다.

v.CNV: 流本营的旗号，按着他们的队伍起行；统领队伍的，是示丢珥的儿子以利蓿。
v.ESV: And the standard of the camp of Reuben set out by their companies, and over their company was Elizur the son of Shedeur.
v.GAE: 다음으로 르우벤 진영의 군기에 속한 자들이 그들의 진영별로 출발하였으니 르우벤의 군대는 스데울의 아들 엘리술이 이끌었고
v.KJV: And the standard of the camp of Reuben set forward according to their armies: and over his host was Elizur the son of Shedeur.
v.NASB: Next the standard of the camp of Reuben, according to their armies, set out with Elizur the son of Shedeur, over its army,
v.NIV: The divisions of the camp of Reuben went next, under their standard. Elizur son of Shedeur was in command.
v.NRSV: Next the standard of the camp of Reuben set out, company by company; and over the whole company was Elizur son of Shedeur.
v.SAENEW: 다음으로는 르우벤 자손이 진의 부대기를 앞세우고, 부대별로 정렬하여 출발하였다. 르우벤 부대는 스데울의 아들 엘리술이 이끌었고,
v.WLB: 그 다음으로 르우벤 진영의 깃발이 그 소속된 부대들을 이끌고 나아갔습니다. 그들은 스데울의 아들 엘리술이 지휘했습니다.

v.CNV: 统领西缅支派的，是苏利沙代的儿子示路蔑。
v.ESV: And over the company of the tribe of the people of Simeon was Shelumiel the son of Zurishaddai.
v.GAE: 시므온 자손 지파의 군대는 수리삿대의 아들 슬루미엘이 이끌었고
v.KJV: And over the host of the tribe of the children of Simeon was Shelumiel the son of Zurishaddai.
v.NASB: and Shelumiel the son of Zurishaddai over the tribal army of the sons of Simeon,
v.NIV: Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai was over the division of the tribe of Simeon,
v.NRSV: Over the company of the tribe of Simeon was Shelumiel son of Zurishaddai,
v.SAENEW: 뒤이어 따라나선 시므온 자손 지파 부대는 수리삿대의 아들 슬루미엘이 이끌었고,
v.WLB: 시므온 지파의 부대는 수리삿대의 아들 슬루미엘이 통솔했습니다.

v.CNV: 统领迦得支派队伍的，是丢珥的儿子以利雅萨。
v.ESV: And over the company of the tribe of the people of Gad was Eliasaph the son of Deuel.
v.GAE: 갓 자손 지파의 군대는 드우엘의 아들 엘리아삽이 이끌었더라
v.KJV: And over the host of the tribe of the children of Gad was Eliasaph the son of Deuel.
v.NASB: and Eliasaph the son of Deuel was over the tribal army of the sons of Gad.
v.NIV: and Eliasaph son of Deuel was over the division of the tribe of Gad.
v.NRSV: and over the company of the tribe of Gad was Eliasaph son of Deuel.
v.SAENEW: 그 다음에 나선 갓 자손 지파 부대는 르우엘의 아들 엘리아삽이 이끌었다.
v.WLB: 갓 지파의 부대는 드우엘의 아들 엘리아삽이 통솔했습니다.

v.CNV: 哥辖人抬着圣物起行；他们到达之前，人已经把帐幕竖立起来。
v.ESV: Then the Kohathites set out, carrying the holy things, and the tabernacle was set up before their arrival.
v.GAE: 고핫인은 성물을 메고 행진하였고 그들이 이르기 전에 성막을 세웠으며
v.KJV: And the Kohathites set forward, bearing the sanctuary: and the other did set up the tabernacle against they came.
v.NASB: Then the Kohathites set out, carrying the holy objects; and the tabernacle was set up before their arrival.
v.NIV: Then the Kohathites set out, carrying the holy things. The tabernacle was to be set up before they arrived.
v.NRSV: Then the Kohathites, who carried the holy things, set out; and the tabernacle was set up before their arrival.
v.SAENEW: 뒤따라 고핫 자손들이 성막 기구들을 메고 출발하였다. 게르손 자손과 므라리 자손은 고핫 자손들이 도착하기 전에 성막을 세워야만 했다.
v.WLB: 그러자 고핫 자손이 거룩한 물품들을 메고 길을 나섰습니다. 성막은 그들이 도착하기 전에 이미 세워져 있어야 했습니다.

v.CNV: 以法莲营的旗号，按着他们的队伍起行；统领队伍的，是亚米忽的儿子以利沙玛。
v.ESV: And the standard of the camp of the people of Ephraim set out by their companies, and over their company was Elishama the son of Ammihud.
v.GAE: 다음으로 에브라임 자손 진영의 군기에 속한 자들이 그들의 진영별로 행진하였으니 에브라임 군대는 암미훗의 아들 엘리사마가 이끌었고
v.KJV: And the standard of the camp of the children of Ephraim set forward according to their armies: and over his host was Elishama the son of Ammihud.
v.NASB: Next the standard of the camp of the sons of Ephraim, according to their armies, was set out, with Elishama the son of Ammihud over its army,
v.NIV: The divisions of the camp of Ephraim went next, under their standard. Elishama son of Ammihud was in command.
v.NRSV: Next the standard of the Ephraimite camp set out, company by company, and over the whole company was Elishama son of Ammihud.
v.SAENEW: 그 다음으로는, 에브라임 자손이 진의 부대기를 앞세우고, 부대별로 정렬하여 출발하였다. 에브라임 부대는 암미훗의 아들 엘리사마가 이끌었고,
v.WLB: 그 다음으로 에브라임 진영의 깃발이 그 소속된 부대들을 이끌고 나아갔습니다. 그들은 암미훗의 아들 엘리사마가 지휘했습니다.

v.CNV: 统领玛拿西支派队伍的，是比大蓿的儿子迦玛列。
v.ESV: And over the company of the tribe of the people of Manasseh was Gamaliel the son of Pedahzur.
v.GAE: 므낫세 자손 지파의 군대는 브다술의 아들 가말리엘이 이끌었고
v.KJV: And over the host of the tribe of the children of Manasseh was Gamaliel the son of Pedahzur.
v.NASB: and Gamaliel the son of Pedahzur over the tribal army of the sons of Manasseh;
v.NIV: Gamaliel son of Pedahzur was over the division of the tribe of Manasseh,
v.NRSV: Over the company of the tribe of Manasseh was Gamaliel son of Pedahzur,
v.SAENEW: 뒤이어 따라나선 므낫세 자손 지파 부대는 브다술의 아들 가말리엘이 이끌었고,
v.WLB: 므낫세 지파의 부대는 브다술의 아들 가말리엘이 통솔했습니다.

v.CNV: 统领便雅悯支派队伍的，是基多尼的儿子亚比但。
v.ESV: And over the company of the tribe of the people of Benjamin was Abidan the son of Gideoni.
v.GAE: 베냐민 자손 지파의 군대는 기드오니의 아들 아비단이 이끌었더라
v.KJV: And over the host of the tribe of the children of Benjamin was Abidan the son of Gideoni.
v.NASB: and Abidan the son of Gideoni over the tribal army of the sons of Benjamin.
v.NIV: and Abidan son of Gideoni was over the division of the tribe of Benjamin.
v.NRSV: and over the company of the tribe of Benjamin was Abidan son of Gideoni.
v.SAENEW: 그 다음에 나선 베냐민 자손 지파 부대는 기드오니의 아들 아비단이 이끌었다.
v.WLB: 베냐민 지파의 부대는 기드오니의 아들 아비단이 통솔했습니다.

v.CNV: 但营的旗号，作众营的后队，按着他们的队伍起行；统领队伍的，是亚米沙代的儿子亚希以谢。
v.ESV: Then the standard of the camp of the people of Dan, acting as the rear guard of all the camps, set out by their companies, and over their company was Ahiezer the son of Ammishaddai.
v.GAE: 다음으로 단 자손 진영의 군기에 속한 자들이 그들의 진영별로 행진하였으니 이 군대는 모든 진영의 마지막 진영이었더라 단 군대는 암미삿대의 아들 아히에셀이 이끌었고
v.KJV: And the standard of the camp of the children of Dan set forward, which was the rereward of all the camps throughout their hosts: and over his host was Ahiezer the son of Ammishaddai.
v.NASB: Then the standard of the camp of the sons of Dan, according to their armies, which formed the rear guard for all the camps, set out, with Ahiezer the son of Ammishaddai over its army,
v.NIV: Finally, as the rear guard for all the units, the divisions of the camp of Dan set out under their standard. Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai was in command.
v.NRSV: Then the standard of the camp of Dan, acting as the rear guard of all the camps, set out, company by company, and over the whole company was Ahiezer son of Ammishaddai.
v.SAENEW: 맨 마지막으로는, 단 자손이 진의 부대기를 앞세우고, 앞선 모든 부대의 후방 경계를 맡은 부대들이 부대별로 정렬하여 출발하였다. 단 부대는 암미삿대의 아들 아히에셀이 이끌었고,
v.WLB: 그 다음으로 단 진영의 깃발이 그 소속된 부대들을 이끌고 모든 진의 후위 부대로서 나아갔습니다. 그들은 암미삿대의 아들 아히에셀이 지휘했습니다.

v.CNV: 统领亚设支派队伍的，是俄兰的儿子帕结。
v.ESV: And over the company of the tribe of the people of Asher was Pagiel the son of Ochran.
v.GAE: 아셀 자손 지파의 군대는 오그란의 아들 바기엘이 이끌었고
v.KJV: And over the host of the tribe of the children of Asher was Pagiel the son of Ocran.
v.NASB: and Pagiel the son of Ochran over the tribal army of the sons of Asher;
v.NIV: Pagiel son of Okran was over the division of the tribe of Asher,
v.NRSV: Over the company of the tribe of Asher was Pagiel son of Ochran,
v.SAENEW: 뒤이어 따라나선 아셀 자손 지파 부대는 오그란의 아들 바기엘이 이끌었고,
v.WLB: 아셀 지파의 부대는 오그란의 아들 바기엘이 통솔했습니다.

v.CNV: 统领拿弗他利支派队伍的，是以南的儿子亚希拉。
v.ESV: And over the company of the tribe of the people of Naphtali was Ahira the son of Enan.
v.GAE: 납달리 자손 지파의 군대는 에난의 아들 아히라가 이끌었더라
v.KJV: And over the host of the tribe of the children of Naphtali was Ahira the son of Enan.
v.NASB: and Ahira the son of Enan over the tribal army of the sons of Naphtali.
v.NIV: and Ahira son of Enan was over the division of the tribe of Naphtali.
v.NRSV: and over the company of the tribe of Naphtali was Ahira son of Enan.
v.SAENEW: 그 다음에 나선 납달리 자손 지파 부대는 에난의 아들 아히라가 이끌었다.
v.WLB: 납달리 지파의 부대는 에난의 아들 아히라가 통솔했습니다.

v.CNV: 这是以色列人起行的时候，按着他们的队伍起行的次序。
v.ESV: This was the order of march of the people of Israel by their companies, when they set out.
v.GAE: 이스라엘 자손이 행진할 때에 이와 같이 그들의 군대를 따라 나아갔더라
v.KJV: Thus were the journeyings of the children of Israel according to their armies, when they set forward.
v.NASB: This was the order of march of the sons of Israel by their armies as they set out.
v.NIV: This was the order of march for the Israelite divisions as they set out.
v.NRSV: This was the order of march of the Israelites, company by company, when they set out.
v.SAENEW: 이것은, 이스라엘 자손이 부대별로 정렬하여 행군할 때의 행군 순서이다.
v.WLB: 이와 같이 이스라엘 자손이 부대별로 행진했습니다.

v.CNV: 摩西对自己的岳父米甸人流珥的儿子何巴说：“我们要起行到耶和华应许的地方去，他曾说：‘我要把那地赐给你们’，现在求你与我们同去，我们必善待你，因为耶和华对以色列已经应许福乐。”
v.ESV: And Moses said to Hobab the son of Reuel the Midianite, Moses' father-in-law, “We are setting out for the place of which the Lord said, ‘I will give it to you.’ Come with us, and we will do good to you, for the Lord has promised good to Israel.”
v.GAE: 모세가 모세의 장인 미디안 사람 르우엘의 아들 호밥에게 이르되 여호와께서 주마 하신 곳으로 우리가 행진하나니 우리와 동행하자 그리하면 선대하리라 여호와께서 이스라엘에게 복을 내리리라 하셨느니라
v.KJV: And Moses said unto Hobab, the son of Raguel the Midianite, Moses’ father in law, We are journeying unto the place of which the LORD said, I will give it you: come thou with us, and we will do thee good: for the LORD hath spoken good concerning Israel.
v.NASB: Then Moses said to Hobab the son of Reuel the Midianite, Moses' father-in-law, 'We are setting out to the place of which the LORD said, 'I will give it to you'; come with us and we will do you good, for the LORD has promised good concerning Israel.'
v.NIV: Now Moses said to Hobab son of Reuel the Midianite, Moses’ father-in-law, “We are setting out for the place about which the Lord said, ‘I will give it to you.’ Come with us and we will treat you well, for the Lord has promised good things to Israel.”
v.NRSV: Moses said to Hobab son of Reuel the Midianite, Moses’ father-in-law, “We are setting out for the place of which the LORD said, ‘I will give it to you’; come with us, and we will treat you well; for the LORD has promised good to Israel.”
v.SAENEW: 모세가 미디안 사람 르우엘의 아들 호밥에게 청하였다. 그는 모세의 장인이었다. "우리는 이제 주님께서 우리에게 주시겠다고 약속하신 곳으로 떠납니다. 장인께서도 우리와 같이 길을 떠나 주시기 바랍니다. 주님께서 이스라엘에게 잘 해주시겠다고 약속하셨으니, 우리가 장인 어른을 잘 대접해 드리겠습니다."
v.WLB: 그때 모세의 장인 미디안 사람 르우엘의 아들 호밥에게 모세가 말했습니다. “우리는 여호와께서 ‘내가 너희에게 주리라’라고 말씀하신 그 장소로 지금 떠나고 있습니다. 우리와 같이 갑시다. 우리가 당신을 잘 대접해 드리겠습니다. 여호와께서 이스라엘에게 좋은 것들을 약속해 주셨습니다.”

v.CNV: 何巴对摩西说：“我不去，我要归回我本地本族那里去。”
v.ESV: But he said to him, “I will not go. I will depart to my own land and to my kindred.”
v.GAE: 호밥이 그에게 이르되 나는 가지 아니하고 내 고향 내 친족에게로 가리라
v.KJV: And he said unto him, I will not go; but I will depart to mine own land, and to my kindred.
v.NASB: But he said to him, 'I will not come, but rather will go to my own land and relatives.'
v.NIV: He answered, “No, I will not go; I am going back to my own land and my own people. ”
v.NRSV: But he said to him, “I will not go, but I will go back to my own land and to my kindred.”
v.SAENEW: 호밥이 모세에게 말하였다. "나는 가지 못하네. 나는 내 고향 내 친척에게로 가야 하네."
v.WLB: 그가 말했습니다. “나는 가지 않겠네. 나는 내 땅과 내 동족에게로 돌아가겠네.”

v.CNV: 摩西说：“求你不要离开我们，因为你知道我们应怎样在旷野安营，你可以作我们的眼目。
v.ESV: And he said, “Please do not leave us, for you know where we should camp in the wilderness, and you will serve as eyes for us.
v.GAE: 모세가 이르되 청하건대 우리를 떠나지 마소서 당신은 우리가 광야에서 어떻게 진 칠지를 아나니 우리의 눈이 되리이다
v.KJV: And he said, Leave us not, I pray thee; forasmuch as thou knowest how we are to encamp in the wilderness, and thou mayest be to us instead of eyes.
v.NASB: Then he said, 'Please do not leave us, inasmuch as you know where we should camp in the wilderness, and you will be as eyes for us.
v.NIV: But Moses said, “Please do not leave us. You know where we should camp in the wilderness, and you can be our eyes.
v.NRSV: He said, “Do not leave us, for you know where we should camp in the wilderness, and you will serve as eyes for us.
v.SAENEW: 모세가 다시 간청하였다. "제발 우리만 버려 두지 마십시오. 우리가 광야 어디에 진을 쳐야 할지, 장인 어른만큼 아는 사람이 없습니다. 그러니 장인께서는 우리의 길 안내자가 되어 주셔야 합니다.
v.WLB: 그러자 모세가 말했습니다. “우리를 떠나지 마십시오. 당신은 우리가 어디에서 진을 쳐야 할지를 알고 있습니다. 우리의 눈이 돼 주십시오.

v.CNV: 如果你与我们同去，将来耶和华用什么好处待我们，我们也必用什么好处待你。”
v.ESV: And if you do go with us, whatever good the Lord will do to us, the same will we do to you.”
v.GAE: 우리와 동행하면 여호와께서 우리에게 복을 내리시는 대로 우리도 당신에게 행하리이다
v.KJV: And it shall be, if thou go with us, yea, it shall be, that what goodness the LORD shall do unto us, the same will we do unto thee.
v.NASB: 'So it will be, if you go with us, that whatever good the LORD does for us, we will do for you.'
v.NIV: If you come with us, we will share with you whatever good things the Lord gives us. ”
v.NRSV: Moreover, if you go with us, whatever good the LORD does for us, the same we will do for you.”
v.SAENEW: 우리와 함께 가시기만 한다면, 주님께서 우리에게 주시는 좋은 것은, 무엇이든지 장인 어른께 나누어 드리겠습니다."
v.WLB: 당신이 우리와 같이 가면 여호와께서 우리에게 주시는 좋은 것을 무엇이든 당신과 나누겠습니다.”

v.CNV: 以色列人从耶和华的山起行，行了三天的路程，耶和华的约柜在他们前面行了三天的路程，为他们寻找安息的地方。
v.ESV: So they set out from the mount of the Lord three days' journey. And the ark of the covenant of the Lord went before them three days' journey, to seek out a resting place for them.
v.GAE: 그들이 여호와의 산에서 떠나 삼 일 길을 갈 때에 여호와의 언약궤가 그 삼 일 길에 앞서 가며 그들의 쉴 곳을 찾았고
v.KJV: And they departed from the mount of the LORD three days’ journey: and the ark of the covenant of the LORD went before them in the three days’ journey, to search out a resting place for them.
v.NASB: Thus they set out from the mount of the LORD three days' journey, with the ark of the covenant of the LORD journeying in front of them for the three days, to seek out a resting place for them.
v.NIV: So they set out from the mountain of the Lord and traveled for three days. The ark of the covenant of the Lord went before them during those three days to find them a place to rest.
v.NRSV: So they set out from the mount of the LORD three days’ journey with the ark of the covenant of the LORD going before them three days’ journey, to seek out a resting place for them,
v.SAENEW: 그들은 주님의 산을 떠나 사흘 길을 갔다. 주님의 언약궤를 앞세우고 사흘 길을 가면서, 쉴 곳을 찾았다.
v.WLB: 그리하여 그들은 여호와의 산을 떠나 3일 길을 나아갔는데 여호와의 언약궤가 쉴 곳을 찾아 3일 동안 그들 앞에서 진행했습니다.

v.CNV: 他们拔营起行的时候，日间总有耶和华的云彩在他们上头。
v.ESV: And the cloud of the Lord was over them by day, whenever they set out from the camp.
v.GAE: 그들이 진영을 떠날 때에 낮에는 여호와의 구름이 그 위에 덮였었더라
v.KJV: And the cloud of the LORD was upon them by day, when they went out of the camp.
v.NASB: The cloud of the LORD was over them by day when they set out from the camp.
v.NIV: The cloud of the Lord was over them by day when they set out from the camp.
v.NRSV: the cloud of the LORD being over them by day when they set out from the camp.
v.SAENEW: 낮이 되어 그들이 진을 떠날 때면, 주님의 구름이 그들 위를 덮어 주었다.
v.WLB: 낮에 그들이 진영을 철수하고 떠날 때 여호와의 구름이 그들 위에 있었습니다.

v.CNV: 约柜起行的时候，摩西就说：“耶和华啊，求你起来，愿你的仇敌四散；愿恨你的人从你面前逃跑。”
v.ESV: And whenever the ark set out, Moses said, “Arise, O Lord , and let your enemies be scattered, and let those who hate you flee before you.”
v.GAE: 궤가 떠날 때에는 모세가 말하되 여호와여 일어나사 주의 대적들을 흩으시고 주를 미워하는 자가 주 앞에서 도망하게 하소서 하였고
v.KJV: And it came to pass, when the ark set forward, that Moses said, Rise up, LORD, and let thine enemies be scattered; and let them that hate thee flee before thee.
v.NASB: Then it came about when the ark set out that Moses said, 'Rise up, O LORD! And let Your enemies be scattered, And let those who hate You flee before You.'
v.NIV: Whenever the ark set out, Moses said, “Rise up, Lord ! May your enemies be scattered; may your foes flee before you. ”
v.NRSV: Whenever the ark set out, Moses would say, “Arise, O LORD, let your enemies be scattered, and your foes flee before you.”
v.SAENEW: 궤가 떠날 때에 모세가 외쳤다. "주님, 일어나십시오. 주님의 원수들을 흩으십시오. 주님을 미워하는 자들을 주님 앞에서 쫓으십시오."
v.WLB: 언약궤가 길을 떠날 때마다 모세가 이렇게 말했습니다. “여호와여 일어나소서! 당신 앞에서 당신의 원수들이 흩어지고 당신의 적들이 달아나게 하소서.”

v.CNV: 约柜停住的时候，他就说：“耶和华啊，求你归来，回到以色列的千万人中。”
v.ESV: And when it rested, he said, “Return, O Lord , to the ten thousand thousands of Israel.”
v.GAE: 궤가 쉴 때에는 말하되 여호와여 이스라엘 종족들에게로 돌아오소서 하였더라
v.KJV: And when it rested, he said, Return, O LORD, unto the many thousands of Israel.
v.NASB: When it came to rest, he said, 'Return, O LORD, To the myriad thousands of Israel.'
v.NIV: Whenever it came to rest, he said, “Return, Lord , to the countless thousands of Israel. ”
v.NRSV: And whenever it came to rest, he would say, “Return, O LORD of the ten thousand thousands of Israel.”
v.SAENEW: 궤가 쉴 때에도 모세가 외쳤다. "주님, 수천만 이스라엘 사람에게로 돌아오십시오."
v.WLB: 그리고 쉴 때마다 이렇게 말했습니다. “여호와여 천만 이스라엘 사람에게로 돌아오소서.”
